title: Define the evidence-preserving resource lifecycle contract
goal: Define a cross-owner lifecycle contract for temporary operational resources: owner, terminal condition, retention, orphan detection, cleanup trigger and retained evidence.

approval.note: User explicitly authorized full registration and implementation on 2026-07-18; live-state, claims, diff review, CI, merge and runtime evidence remain mandatory.

acceptance.assertion: Tasks, claims, leases, worktrees, workers, caches, outboxes, bundles, profiles, feature flags and staging states are classified.
Each resource type names authoritative terminal evidence; time or process absence alone cannot erase history.
Cleanup is idempotent, race-safe, owner-bound and preserves immutable evidence.
Existing Bureau, Grabowski and Chronik lifecycle mechanisms have explicit compatible migration steps.

does_not_establish: authority outside the listed source systems
